content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. For each kkk (1‚Č§k‚Č§N1 \le k \le N1‚Č§k‚Č§N), let's define a function f(k)f(k)f(k) in the following way:


Consider a sequence B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč, which is created by setting Ak=0A_k = 0Ak‚Äč=0. Formally, Bk=0B_k = 0Bk‚Äč=0 and Bi=AiB_i = A_iBi‚Äč=Ai‚Äč for each valid i‚Č†ki \neq kiÓÄ†=k.
f(k)f(k)f(k) is the number of ways to split the sequence BBB into two non-empty contiguous subsequences with equal sums.
Find the sum S=f(1)+f(2)+‚Ä¶+f(N)S = f(1) + f(2) + \ldots + f(N)S=f(1)+f(2)+‚Ä¶+f(N).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the sum SSS.
